name: Cookie Cutters Haircuts for Kids
description: Stylists trained to work with children with sensory sensitivities. TVs and fun chairs to make haircuts enjoyable.

name: Kiddie Kutters
description: Patient stylists experienced with children on the autism spectrum. Will use scissors instead of clippers for sensory-sensitive kids.

name: Komb's Studio
description: Gentle stylists who accommodate sensory needs — scissors-only cuts, quiet appointment times, and a patient approach.

name: Venus Salon
description: Autism-friendly salon providing a calm, accommodating environment for kids. Serving Colorado Springs for 14+ years.

name: Family Support Center
description: Offers free haircuts for children with autism. Contact Kearstie Pearce to schedule.

name: Care and Share Food Bank
description: Southern Colorado's leading food bank. Operates Sunny Side Markets — free pantries with fresh produce, meats, dairy, and more.

name: Catholic Charities — Marian House
description: Free hot meals served daily, 365 days a year (10:30 AM–1 PM). The Marketplace offers monthly grocery shopping for families.

name: Salvation Army Food Pantry
description: Nonperishable staples and fresh produce. Open Wed & Fri, 10 AM–2 PM. Bring valid ID.

name: Mercy's Gate
description: Emergency food assistance — 3-day supply per household member. Pantry organized like a mini store. Up to 4 visits per year.

name: Pikes Peak United Way
description: Family Success Center hosting Sunny Side Markets plus resources to help families achieve financial stability.

name: Play Street Museum
description: Sensory-friendly playtime every Wednesday 12:30–2 PM. Limited attendance and reduced sensory stimuli.

name: We Rock the Spectrum
description: Indoor playground designed with children on the autism spectrum in mind. Inclusive equipment, events, and classes for ages 0–13.

name: Air City 360
description: Special Needs Family Night every Monday 3–8 PM. Sensory room available plus in-person discounts.

name: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo
description: Sensory bags with noise-canceling headphones, fidget tools, and communication cards. Quiet zones available throughout.

name: SafeSplash Swim School
description: Adaptive swim lessons led by trained instructors in a calm, supportive environment for all abilities.

name: Pioneers Museum — Sensory Mornings
description: Reduced lighting and noise with hands-on activities for a more inclusive museum experience.

name: Inclusive Playgrounds
description: Panorama Park, Swing High at Memorial Park, and The Corral at John Venezia Park feature wheelchair-accessible, sensory-friendly equipment.

name: The Arc Pikes Peak Region
description: Advocates for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities. IEP/504 meeting support and educational advocacy.

name: Special Kids Special Families
description: Respite care, adult day programs, foster care, and host home programs for individuals with disabilities across their lifespan.

name: PEAK Parent Center
description: Statewide training and resources empowering parents to advocate for their children's educational needs and rights.

name: Disability Law Colorado
description: Legal representation, advocacy, and education protecting the rights of people with disabilities.

name: The Resource Exchange
description: Serves El Paso and Teller Counties. Early intervention services, Medicaid waiver coordination for 9,000+ individuals.
